skill.name: hexagonal-advisor
skill.description: Reviews code architecture for hexagonal patterns, checks dependency directions, and suggests improvements for ports and adapters separation. Activates when users work with services, repositories, or architectural patterns.
skill.summary: Reviews Rust code for hexagonal architecture boundaries, ports, adapters, and dependency direction.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment access, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and obfuscation indicators. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences and Rust architecture examples; the skill declares only Read, Grep, and Glob tools and contains no executable scripts or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Rust Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to fenced Rust examples and suggestion templates, not shell commands or executable skill logic. The skill file is instructional Markdown and the declared tools are read-only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fences containing Rust snippets for architecture guidance. No shell interpreter, subprocess call, or executable file is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example Database URL Usage
low_findings.description: The database connection string finding refers to a Rust composition-root example that connects with a configuration value. The skill does not read environment variables or collect credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a non-executed Rust example showing dependency wiring. There is no evidence of environment access, secret handling, or data exfiltration by the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example Server Bind Address
low_findings.description: The hardcoded IP finding is an illustrative Axum server bind address in a sample application. It is not a network request made by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The address appears only inside an example main function for application composition. The skill itself has no network tool permission or network client code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Heuristic
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match ordinary words and Rust serialization identifiers, such as the file description and derive examples. No cryptographic API or algorithm is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other cryptographic implementation. The flagged lines are metadata text and a Rust derive example.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Obfuscation and Reconnaissance Heuristics
low_findings.description: The obfuscation and reconnaissance findings are caused by Markdown structure, Rust type syntax, and a SQL SELECT example. No encoded payload, JavaScript obfuscation, or host reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown with Rust code examples and no JavaScript runtime or system command usage. The suspicious combination is not semantically present.

content.value_statement: Rust services often mix domain logic with database, HTTP, and framework details. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code review ports, adapters, and dependency direction.
actual_capabilities: Identifies direct infrastructure dependencies inside domain and service code.
Suggests Rust port traits for repositories, services, and external integrations.
Reviews adapter implementations for correct domain-to-infrastructure boundaries.
Highlights domain models that expose database, framework, or serialization concerns.
Recommends mockable testing patterns for services that depend on ports.
Explains composition-root wiring for repositories, services, and HTTP handlers.
limitations: Focused on Rust examples and may need adaptation for other languages.
Provides architectural guidance, not automated refactoring or compilation checks.
Does not inspect repositories unless the host assistant reads the relevant files.
May need project-specific context before judging intentional boundary exceptions.

use_cases.title: Review a New Rust Service
use_cases.description: Check whether a service keeps business rules separate from databases, HTTP clients, and framework types.

use_cases.title: Plan a Refactor
use_cases.description: Turn tightly coupled repository or service code into ports, adapters, and a clear composition root.

use_cases.title: Improve Testability
use_cases.description: Find places where domain logic requires real infrastructure and suggest mockable port traits.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Architecture Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one service or repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust service for hexagonal architecture issues. Focus on dependency direction, ports, adapters, and testability.

prompt_templates.title: Port Design Help
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest port traits for this Rust domain logic. Keep the traits domain-focused and avoid database, HTTP, or framework types.

prompt_templates.title: Adapter Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether infrastructure code implements ports cleanly.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Rust adapters against their ports. Identify leaky abstractions, misplaced business logic, and missing conversions.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced migration from coupled code to ports and adapters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an incremental refactoring plan for moving this Rust module toward hexagonal architecture. Include testing strategy and composition-root changes.

output_examples.input: A UserService owns a database pool and runs SQL during user creation.
output_examples.output: The review flags a dependency direction problem. It recommends a UserRepository port, a service that depends on that port, and a Postgres adapter that owns SQL.

output_examples.input: A domain User type derives database row and serialization traits.
output_examples.output: The review explains that persistence concerns leaked into the domain. It suggests a separate adapter row type and conversion at the boundary.

output_examples.input: A service can only be tested with a real database.
output_examples.output: The review recommends a mock repository implementation and domain-level tests that exercise behavior without infrastructure.

best_practices: Share the relevant service, repository, domain model, and adapter files together.
Ask for small refactoring steps when the current design is tightly coupled.
Include testing goals so recommendations favor practical seams for mocks.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to replace compiler errors, benchmarks, or integration tests.
Do not apply generic port traits without checking real domain language.
Do not treat every framework dependency as wrong without considering boundaries.

faq.question: What architecture style does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews hexagonal architecture, also called ports and adapters, with a focus on Rust code.

faq.question: Can it rewrite my project automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review notes, examples, and refactoring plans that an assistant or developer can apply.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill declares read-only file inspection tools and does not need network access.

faq.question: Can it help with tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests mock implementations and test structures that avoid real infrastructure when testing domain logic.

faq.question: Is it only for Rust?
faq.answer: The guidance and examples are written for Rust. The concepts can inform other languages, but examples may need translation.

faq.question: What files should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide domain models, service implementations, repository traits, adapters, and application wiring files.
